phases.name: Positioning
phases.description: Define project scope and separation from bridge

phases.name: Extraction
phases.description: Resilient data extraction from unstable inputs

tasks.label: Partial read support
tasks.note: _read_drawers_resilient returns partial results; tested by test_good_rows_survive_bad_rows and test_partial_*

tasks.label: Invalid JSON tolerance
tasks.note: Extraction is SQLite-typed (no JSON parsing). Document/metadata strings are returned as-is. Tested by test_document_*json* and test_metadata_json_like_*.

tasks.label: Corrupted SQLite handling
tasks.note: PRAGMA integrity_check failure and missing required tables raise ExtractionError (CRITICAL). Mid-scan page error caught by while/next loop. Tested.

tasks.label: Record-level isolation
tasks.note: Each row processed independently; failure appended to failed_rows without affecting others. Tested by all _excluded tests.

tasks.label: No global crash
tasks.note: Pre-flight critical failures raise ExtractionError (not bare exceptions). Per-row failures isolated. Mid-scan DatabaseError caught and returns partial results. Tested by test_mid_scan_corruption_returns_partial.

tasks.label: Structured anomalies output
tasks.note: ctx.add_anomaly() called for every failure with type/severity/stage/message/context. FailedRow.to_dict() tested. Tested by test_anomaly_emitted_* suite.

tasks.label: Define anomaly enum types
tasks.note: AnomalyType (str, Enum) defined in core/context.py as a closed registry of every anomaly type the system can emit. Free-form type strings are rejected by add_anomaly via _coerce_type.

tasks.label: Define severity levels
tasks.note: Severity (str, Enum) with LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL. Invalid severities rejected by _coerce_severity. SEVERITIES tuple kept for stable ordering. Severity.HIGH == 'high' preserved for backward-compat string equality.

tasks.label: Define location schema
tasks.note: AnomalyLocation dataclass with required non-empty stage and optional source/identifier/record_pk/path/extra. Anomaly.stage delegates to location.stage.

tasks.label: Attach evidence model
tasks.note: AnomalyEvidence dataclass (kind, detail, data). Anomaly.__post_init__ enforces at least one evidence entry. All extraction and pipeline call sites migrated to attach structured evidence.

tasks.label: Remove unstructured logs
tasks.note: ctx.anomalies is the single channel for inconsistencies. No print/logging calls in extraction or pipeline. Detection still has its own structured Evidence/Contradiction model (predates M3 and remains structurally represented). Legacy add_anomaly(stage=..., context=...) shape still accepted but produces a structurally-valid Anomaly with synthesized AnomalyLocation + AnomalyEvidence(kind='legacy_context').

phases.name: Reporting
phases.description: Expose full system state and anomalies

tasks.label: Global stats
tasks.note: extraction_stats (total/parsed/failed/rate) + stages section (executed|aborted|skipped|not_run per stage) in build_report().

tasks.label: Anomaly aggregation
tasks.note: anomaly_summary extended with by_stage, top_severity (highest observed severity). by_type already existed. Derived from final anomaly_dicts list so consistency-invariant meta-anomaly is included.

tasks.label: Confidence summary
tasks.note: confidence_summary section: detection.band from DetectionResult.confidence_band, extraction.band from parse_rate thresholds, overall_band = weakest_band(detection, extraction). UNKNOWN when any signal is absent. Rule is explicit and documented.

tasks.label: Human-readable summary
tasks.note: reporting/text_renderer.py: pure render_text(report) function. CLI delegates entirely; no rendering logic in cli/.

tasks.label: Structured JSON output
tasks.note: schema_version=3. REPORT_TOP_LEVEL_KEYS tuple pins the contract. All sections are JSON-safe (tested by test_report_is_json_safe_strict without default=).

tasks.label: Structural validation
tasks.note: validation/structural.py: 3 checks (extraction_arithmetic, drawer_shapes, detection_evidence_nonempty). Severity cap HIGH. Verifies upstream object shapes without interpreting contents.

tasks.label: Internal consistency checks
tasks.note: validation/consistency.py: 4 checks (unique_drawer_ids, id_not_in_both_parsed_and_failed, failed_row_has_anomaly which externally verifies the M3 guarantee, stage_result_coherence).

tasks.label: Heuristic plausibility
tasks.note: validation/heuristics.py: 3 checks with module-level threshold constants (PARSE_RATE_PLAUSIBILITY_FLOOR=0.50, DOMINANT_FAILURE_TYPE_THRESHOLD=0.80). Severity capped at MEDIUM by design — heuristics never produce HIGH/CRITICAL.

tasks.label: Confidence-based output
tasks.note: ValidationResult.confidence_band ∈ {HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, UNKNOWN} via weakest-outcome rule. Integrated into report confidence_summary; overall_band = weakest_band(detection, extraction, validation).

tasks.label: Avoid 'correctness' claims
tasks.note: Trichotomous CheckStatus (passed/failed/inconclusive) — no boolean returns. checks_not_performed always populated (target_record_count_parity, target_id_set_parity remain skipped while reconstruction is a stub). Forbidden-vocabulary regex test enforces no 'correct/verified/guaranteed/safe/valid' in JSON output. validate() never raises MigratorError.

tasks.label: Input handling
tasks.note: click.Path(exists=True, dir_okay=True) on source arg; report subcommand accepts existing file. Nonexistent path rejected by Click before pipeline entry.

tasks.label: Output display
tasks.note: --json-output global flag: JSON to stdout only. Text via render_text(). --quiet suppresses all output while preserving exit code. _decide_exit_code() is the single authority on exit codes; exit 8 guards silent CRITICAL anomalies.

tasks.label: Execution modes
tasks.note: Three subcommands: analyze (ANALYZE_PIPELINE: detect+extract), inspect (FULL_PIPELINE: +transform/reconstruct stubs+validate), report (re-render saved JSON, no pipeline). PIPELINES registry in core/pipeline.py. No migrate/reconstruct commands.

tasks.label: Exit-code policy (_decide_exit_code)
tasks.note: Pinned codes: 0=ok, 2=detect, 3=extract, 4=transform, 5=reconstruct, 6=report, 7=validate, 8=success-but-critical-anomaly, 10=unexpected. Pure function; unit-tested in test_cli.py::TestDecideExitCode (11 cases).

phases.name: Adversarial Testing
phases.description: Break the system intentionally. Strategy fixed in tests/M7_ADVERSARIAL_DESIGN.md. No new stages, exit codes, severities, or features may be added by M7. Defects discovered are fixed in the owning production module, not absorbed in test code.

tasks.label: Corrupted JSON / blob cases (extraction-row level)
tasks.asserts: per-row anomaly with evidence.kind naming the bad column; row in failed_rows, not in parsed; exit 0 or 8; never crash.
tasks.note: 7 corpus entries; per-row contracts pinned for blank_embedding_id / control_chars_in_id / document_missing / document_null_string_value. JSON-agnostic cases (unparseable_metadata_string_value, document_multiple, metadata_all_null) are skipped here and covered by 10.6 invariants.

tasks.label: Broken SQLite cases (pre-flight + mid-scan)
tasks.asserts: detection or extraction rejection -> exit 2 or 3; CRITICAL anomaly attributed to the failing stage; no Python traceback escapes to stderr.
tasks.note: Detection's structural pre-scan catches sqlite_missing / zeroed_header / required_table_missing / multiple_collections / unexpected_collection_name BEFORE extraction; this is correct early-rejection behavior. Truncated SQLite remains outcome-dependent.

tasks.label: Mixed-format / contradictory-signal inputs
tasks.asserts: failure.stage='detect' on exit 2; detection records contradictions OR an inconsistency-kind evidence record OR drops band below HIGH; no silent classification.

tasks.label: Inconsistent data (duplicates / cross-set membership / collection mismatch)
tasks.asserts: validation summary_counts.failed + inconclusive > 0 on broken inspect runs; validation never silently claims all-passed; band != HIGH; an extract-stage anomaly explains the downgrade.

tasks.label: Extreme edge cases (empty, 0-byte, manifest-only, empty-palace inspect)
tasks.asserts: every edge case reaches a modelled exit code (0/2/3); non-zero exits emit at least one anomaly and populate report.failure with a non-empty stage; never exit 10; never raw traceback on stderr without --debug.

tasks.label: Cross-cutting invariants over the full adversarial corpus (the actual exit gate)
tasks.asserts: nine invariants hold for every corpus entry: no exit 10, no traceback w/o --debug, no silent CRITICAL, schema_version==REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION, JSON-safe without default=, anomaly well-formedness, forbidden-vocabulary regex, stage attribution on raised errors, stdout/stderr discipline.
tasks.note: M7 surfaced one production defect: detection evidence string contained the forbidden word 'valid' on manifest JSON parse failure; fixed in src/mempalace_migrator/detection/format_detector.py. All 280 invariant assertions green across 28 corpus entries.

phases.name: Final Hardening
phases.description: Lock the runtime envelope (logging, latency, memory, repeatability) of the M1–M7 system as a recorded baseline so future drift is detected, not discovered. M8 adds NO new pipeline stages, NO new exit codes, NO new severities, NO new AnomalyType values, NO new CLI flags, NO production print/logging. Strategy fixed in tests/M8_HARDENING_DESIGN.md.
phases.implementation_status: done — tests/hardening/ implemented, baselines committed, 718 tests green

tasks.label: Logging discipline (structural ban via AST walk)
tasks.asserts: no print / logging / sys.std*.write call site under src/mempalace_migrator/; click.echo call sites confined to a documented allow-list in cli/main.py; --quiet stdout empty; --json-output stdout is exactly one JSON document.
tasks.note: Production code is currently free of these call sites (verified during M8 design pass). M8.1 turns the current good state into a pinned structural contract via an ast walk — NOT a regex — so aliased imports (from logging import getLogger as gl) are also caught.

tasks.label: Performance baseline (drift detector, not benchmark)
tasks.asserts: for every entry in baselines/runtime_envelope.json: median wall-clock over 5 subprocess runs <= recorded_p50 * (1 + tolerance.wall_clock_pct/100). Default tolerance: 50%.
tasks.note: Subprocess only — never CliRunner. Absolute wall-clock numbers are forbidden; only delta-vs-baseline is asserted. No flake-retry. Re-baseline only via tests/hardening/rebaseline.py in a reviewed PR.

tasks.label: Memory envelope (peak RSS via getrusage)
tasks.asserts: for every entry in baselines/runtime_envelope.json: observed_peak_rss <= recorded_peak_rss * (1 + tolerance.rss_bytes_pct/100). Default tolerance: 25%.
tasks.note: POSIX-only via resource.getrusage(RUSAGE_CHILDREN). Non-POSIX skip is asserted (no silent skips). tracemalloc inside the test process is forbidden — the CLI runs in a subprocess.

tasks.label: Stability invariants (the actual M8 exit gate)
tasks.asserts: nine invariants over the baseline corpus: report-signature stability, schema stability, exit-code stability, wall-clock envelope, RSS envelope, logging discipline (re-asserted), stdout/stderr discipline (re-asserted), all M7 invariants still hold on the baseline subset, determinism (two consecutive runs produce identical signature modulo run_id/started_at).
tasks.note: Imports M7 invariants from a refactored tests/adversarial/_invariants.py module — test-only refactor, no M7 assertion changes. Drift fails the suite; there is no --update-baseline flag.

current_focus.label: All milestones complete — M8 exit gate satisfied
current_focus.reason: M8 exit gate is satisfied. tests/hardening/ implemented with 4 sub-task files (11.1–11.4), tests/adversarial/_invariants.py (shared check functions), tests/hardening/rebaseline.py, and committed baseline files. Full suite green at 718 passed. All nine M7 invariants re-enforced on the 11-entry baseline corpus. AST walk finds zero forbidden call sites in src/. Two-run determinism check passes. Wall-clock and RSS envelopes pass with fresh-subprocess RUSAGE_CHILDREN isolation.
